evals.prompt: Write a Python Flask endpoint that handles user signup: validate the email, check password is at least 8 chars, hash it with bcrypt rounds of 12, set session expiry to 3600 seconds, and return a 201 on success or 400/409 on errors
evals.expected_output: Code that uses named constants for magic numbers (MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH, BCRYPT_ROUNDS, SESSION_EXPIRY_SECONDS, HTTP status codes) rather than inline literals

assertions.name: no_inline_password_length
assertions.description: Password min length (8) is a named constant, not an inline literal in the comparison

assertions.name: no_inline_bcrypt_rounds
assertions.description: Bcrypt rounds (12) is a named constant, not an inline literal

assertions.name: no_inline_session_expiry
assertions.description: Session expiry (3600) is a named constant, not an inline literal

assertions.name: no_inline_status_codes
assertions.description: HTTP status codes (201, 400, 409) use named constants or are clearly not magic (e.g. returned directly in a tuple, not embedded in logic)

assertions.name: constants_grouped
assertions.description: All constants are defined together at the top of the file or in a dedicated config section

evals.prompt: Add three Express route handlers: GET /users/:id, GET /orders/:id, and GET /products/:id. Each should fetch the item from the database, return 404 if not found, check if the requester has permission, return 403 if not, and return the item as JSON.
evals.expected_output: Code that extracts the repeated fetch-validate-authorize-respond pattern into a shared helper function rather than duplicating it across all three handlers

assertions.name: has_shared_helper
assertions.description: A reusable helper function or middleware handles the repeated fetch-check-auth-respond pattern

assertions.name: no_duplicated_fetch_logic
assertions.description: The fetch-and-check-existence logic is NOT copy-pasted across the three handlers

assertions.name: no_duplicated_auth_logic
assertions.description: Permission checking is NOT copy-pasted across the three handlers

assertions.name: handlers_are_concise
assertions.description: Each route handler is concise and delegates to the shared helper rather than reimplementing the pattern

evals.prompt: Write a Python script that connects to Redis at localhost:6379 with db=2 and a 5-second timeout, then connects to Postgres at localhost:5432/myapp with pool size 10, and sets up a logger with max file size 10485760 and 5 backup files
evals.expected_output: Code that extracts connection strings, ports, timeouts, pool sizes, and file sizes into named constants or a config object rather than scattering literals throughout the code

assertions.name: config_centralized
assertions.description: Connection params (hosts, ports, timeouts, pool sizes) are in a config object or constants block, not scattered inline in function calls

assertions.name: no_inline_port_numbers
assertions.description: Port numbers (6379, 5432) are not bare literals in connection calls

assertions.name: no_inline_file_size
assertions.description: The log file size (10485760) is a named constant, not an inline literal

assertions.name: no_inline_timeout
assertions.description: Timeout (5) and backup count (5) are named constants, not bare literals

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript function for a shopping cart component: it tracks currentCount of items, shows an empty state when there are no items, enables the checkout button when there are items, shows a 'last item!' warning when only one remains, and disables the 'remove' button when the cart is empty. Also handle a search results list that shows 'no results' when indexOf returns a not-found sentinel.
evals.expected_output: Code that uses named constants or helper functions for boundary checks (=== 0, > 0, === 1, === -1) instead of raw literals scattered across comparisons

assertions.name: no_raw_zero_checks
assertions.description: Comparisons for 'empty' state (=== 0) use a named constant like EMPTY or a helper like isEmpty(), not raw 0 in multiple places

assertions.name: no_raw_positive_checks
assertions.description: Comparisons for 'has items' (> 0) use a named constant or helper like hasItems(), not raw 0 in multiple places

assertions.name: no_raw_negative_one
assertions.description: The indexOf not-found check (=== -1) uses a named constant like NOT_FOUND, not raw -1

assertions.name: boundary_constants_or_helpers
assertions.description: Boundary values are centralized — either as constants at the top or as reusable helper functions
